name: Architect Breakdown Task Breakout
description: Two-stage workflow that proves an architect can author a breakdown and the orchestrator can turn it into aligned implementation work.
outcome: A design note and structured work breakdown lead to three implementation outputs that each say hi back.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `solution-design` and `implementation`. Treat `solution-design` as the point where the workflow decides what work exists next, and treat `implementation` as the point where the orchestrator must turn that design output into concrete downstream work items and tasks. Use these roles deliberately: `architect-breakdown-designer` owns the design and decomposition step; `chunk-delivery-finisher` owns each implementation chunk and only needs to produce the small authored output for that chunk.
Happy path: start in `solution-design`, dispatch `architect-breakdown-designer`, and require that role to read the repository brief, write both `docs/design-note.md` and `docs/work-breakdown.json`, verify the JSON structure directly, upload the work-breakdown artifact, and submit one full handoff. That handoff MUST include a machine-readable list of recommended work splits in generic `role_data`, and it MUST cite the repo-relative breakdown path so the orchestrator can inspect it. After that handoff, the orchestrator MUST read the architect output, create one `implementation` work item per recommended split using the exact authored chunk title, and route `chunk-delivery-finisher` for each created chunk.
For `implementation`, `chunk-delivery-finisher` must stay scoped to its current chunk. The specialist must read the assigned chunk title and any cited breakdown details, write `deliverables/<chunk-id>.txt` containing the exact phrase `hi back`, run `./scripts/verify.sh <chunk-id>`, upload the resulting file as an artifact, and submit a full handoff. The orchestrator must keep creating or routing the remaining chunk work until every authored split has a corresponding completed implementation work item and verified output.
Unhappy path: If the architect omits a required split, produces malformed JSON, or leaves the machine-readable breakdown inconsistent with the design note, keep `solution-design` open and route corrected design work instead of guessing the missing breakdown. If an implementation chunk fails verification, reroute only that chunk back to `chunk-delivery-finisher` with a precise correction brief. Do not invent approvals, assessments, or escalations for this playbook unless the repository brief or verification path is genuinely missing. Do not skip a missing chunk just because the other chunks are complete, and do not create implementation work from vague prose when the authored breakdown artifact or structured handoff payload is incomplete.
Completion rules: finish only after `architect-breakdown-designer` has produced the verified design outputs, every recommended chunk from the architect's breakdown has a matching `implementation` work item, every such work item has been executed by `chunk-delivery-finisher`, and the repository contains the final chunk outputs. Guided closure rule: treat the authored flow as best-intent process guidance. Preferred routing is architect first and then one implementation task per authored chunk, but the orchestrator may waive a preferred detail with a recorded reason when closure is still legally possible. Only explicit blockers grounded in missing repository inputs, malformed authored breakdown data, or failed chunk verification should keep the workflow open; non-blocking deviations should be recorded as callouts instead of preventing closure.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: solution-design
stages.goal: A verified design note and machine-readable work breakdown exist.

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: Each authored chunk has a verified output.
